page_content: Candidate name Amarnath Sm Job Title Sr. Software Engineer - Fullstack Date Friday, February 27th, 2026 Assessment Interview score 8 OUT OF 10 AI Summary Technical Skill 4 / 5 Problem Solving 3 / 5 Code Quality 3 / 5 Communication Rating 4 / 5 Interview Summary Language/Framework… 4 Software Engineering 3 Frontend Concepts 3 8 OUT OF 10

HIGHLIGHTS The candidate demonstrates strong backend fundamentals in C#/.NET along with working knowledge of React and frontend concepts. Shows practical understanding of concurrency concepts such as thread pool starvation, async blocking calls, ConcurrentDictionary, and Interlocked. Has hands-on experience in API design, versioning, pagination, idempotency concepts, and cloud exposure (S3, Lambda, Terraform deployments). Some gaps remain in distributed system concepts (API Gateway, blue-green deployment, optimistic vs pessimistic locking), but overall performance aligns well with expectations for a strong IC fullstack engineer. Suitable for Senior Software Engineer – Fullstack role. Highlights • Good explanation of thread pool starvation scenarios (.Wait/.Result causing blocking). • Understands non-blocking async and importance of await

. • Understands non-blocking async and importance of await. • Strong grasp of ConcurrentDictionary and shared resource updates. • Knows usage of Interlocked for atomic operations. • Correct understanding of idempotency and API contracts(high level). • Able to explain pagination design clearly. • Good React fundamentals (re-render triggers, Virtual DOM, memoization). • Exposure to AWS hosting, Terraform, Jenkins deployments. SCOPE OF IMPROVEMENT • Could not clearly differentiate race condition vs deadlock. • Async deadlock explanation lacked depth (focused mostly on sync context). • Blue-green deployment unfamiliar. • API Gateway explanation missing. • Locking strategies (optimistic vs pessimistic) unclear. • Limited clarity on how rate limiting is enforced technically. • Reconciliation explanation in React was partial

. • Limited clarity on how rate limiting is enforced technically. • Reconciliation explanation in React was partial. Language/Framework Fundamentals C# ENTITY FRAMEWORK MVC 4 Solid Performance, Exceeds Expectations OVERALL FEEDBACK • Strong C# fundamentals. • Good understanding of thread pool starvation and blocking calls. • Explained ConcurrentDictionary correctly for concurrent updates. OUT OF 5

• Understood Interlocked usage for atomic increments. • Knows async best practices (avoid blocking calls like .Wait/.Result). • Slight confusion in race condition vs deadlock differentiation. • MVC, WCF, ASP.NET experience . • Testing experience present (though not deeply probed in this round). Overall: Strong backend language fundamentals suitable for senior IC role. Result, high performing tasks and all blocking calls , non blocking calss and always await , static values , u can use interlocked , in async operations , dll , don't need to manage execution context ConcurrentDictionary - lock management system , locks for every thread , resources are shared but can be updated concurrently , When should you use it - updating simultaneously , complex Value fi Interlocked - increent static values , value is shared and multiple increase it How do you debug race conditionsfi - deadlock where you cannot recover , 2 objects accessing at same time , example race condition - one using it another is trying to use race condition vs deadlock - not sure what was the situation - What causes deadlocks in async codefi he said deadlock occurs in sync

what is sync code - Software Engineering 3 Performance Meets Expectations OVERALL FEEDBACK REST API design understanding solid. • Correct explanation of PUT vs PATCH. • Good understanding of idempotency concept. • Pagination design explained clearly. • Rate limiting concept known but enforcement mechanics unclear. • Hosting knowledge via IIS and AWS (Terraform + Jenkins). • AWS exposure (S3, Lambda) present but limited hands-on testing clarity. • API Gateway concept not clearly articulated. • Blue-green deployment unfamiliar. • Locking strategy knowledge limited. Overall: Strong application-level engineering, moderate distributed systems depth

. • Locking strategy knowledge limited. Overall: Strong application-level engineering, moderate distributed systems depth.
